summary: Verified noscript removal fix on preview URL. All 12 routes serve 0 noscript elements and 0 'enable JavaScript' text. Body opens directly with <div id="root"><div (only an HTML comment intervening, which is invisible to text extractors). SSG content intact: wrap-rage > 20KB with essay body, marketing-writes-checks > 25KB, home > 13KB with 'Most B2B firms are better'. Per-route meta (title/canonical/og/twitter) present and route-specific. Browser test on /writing/wrap-rage: 0 console errors, 0 hydration warnings, client-side nav updates document.title correctly. Netlify /writing/wrap-rage/ also verified 200, >20KB, contains essay. 42/42 pytest tests pass.
critical_code_review_comments: public/index.html has a helpful HTML comment (lines 53-58) explaining why noscript is absent — good documentation for future contributors, and comments are invisible to text extractors so they don't reintroduce the original issue.
Netlify's 301 redirect from no-trailing-slash to trailing-slash is noted but not tested here; requests follows redirects transparently and standard crawlers handle it fine — non-issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_17: verified noscript removal. Preview URL clean, SSG intact, per-route meta intact, hydration clean. User next step: push to GitHub so Netlify picks up the noscript-removal fix (current Netlify deploy may still contain noscript until push). Regression test file at /app/backend/tests/test_noscript_removal.py covers all 12 routes and can be re-run against Netlify by swapping PREVIEW→NETLIFY.
rca of the issue: Original bug: CRA's default public/index.html shipped with <noscript>You need to enable JavaScript to run this app.</noscript> inside <body> BEFORE <div id="root">. Even though the site is now fully SSG'd (content present in initial HTML), some HTML text extractors read the first-visible-text and hit that noscript message. Fix (already applied by main agent): removed the noscript element entirely from public/index.html and rebuilt. Verified in build output and served responses.
